2604.13448.title: A Study of Failure Modes in Two-Stage Human-Object Interaction Detection
2604.13448.paper_summary: Human-object interaction (HOI) detection aims to detect interactions between humans and objects in images. While recent advances have improved performance on existing benchmarks, their evaluations mainly focus on overall prediction accuracy and provide limited insight into the underlying causes of model failures. In particular, modern models often struggle in complex scenes involving multiple people and rare interaction combinations. In this work, we present a study to better understand the failure modes of two-stage HOI models, which form the basis of many current HOI detection approaches. Rather than constructing a large-scale benchmark, we instead decompose HOI detection into multiple interpretable perspectives and analyze model behavior across these dimensions to study different types of failure patterns. We curate a subset of images from an existing HOI dataset organized by human-object-interaction configurations (e.g., multi-person interactions and object sharing), and analyze model behavior under these configurations to examine different failure modes. This design allows us to analyze how these HOI models behave under different scene compositions and why their predictions fail. Importantly, high overall benchmark performance does not necessarily reflect robust visual reasoning about human-object relationships. We hope that this study can provide useful insights into the limitations of HOI models and offer observations for future research in this area.
2604.13448.paper_summary_zh: 人類-物體互動（HOI）檢測旨在檢測圖像中人類與物體之間的互動。雖然最近的進展提高了現有基準的性能，但它們的評估主要集中在整體預測準確性上，並對模型失敗的根本原因提供有限的洞見。特別是，現代模型在涉及多個人和稀有互動組合的複雜場景中經常面臨挑戰。在這項工作中，我們提出了一項研究，以更好地理解兩階段 HOI 模型的失敗模式，這些模型是許多當前 HOI 檢測方法的基礎。我們不是構建一個大規模基準，而是將 HOI 檢測分解為多個可解釋的視角，並分析模型在這些維度上的行為，以研究不同類型的失敗模式。我們從現有的 HOI 數據集中策劃了一個圖像子集，這些圖像按人類-物體-互動配置（例如，多人互動和物體共享）進行組織，並分析模型在這些配置下的行為，以檢查不同的失敗模式。這種設計使我們能夠分析這些 HOI 模型在不同場景組合下的行為，以及為什麼它們的預測會失敗。重要的是，高整體基準性能不一定反映出對人類-物體關係的穩健視覺推理。我們希望這項研究能為 HOI 模型的局限性提供有用的洞見，並為未來在該領域的研究提供觀察。
2604.13448.author: Lemeng Wang et.al.
2604.13448.authors: Lemeng Wang, Qinqian Lei, Vidhi Bakshi, Daniel Yi, Yifan Liu, Jiacheng Hou, Asher Seng Hao, Zheda Mai, Wei-Lun Chao, Robby T. Tan, Bo Wang
